name: Windows Defender Uninstall via PowerShell
description: Detects the use of PowerShell to uninstall Windows Defender. PowerShell is a powerful scripting tool, and this command is a strong indicator of malicious activity, as adversaries often attempt to disable or uninstall security products to evade detection. Microsoft Defender Antivirus is a critical anti-malware component of Windows.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that executed the command, which should be 'powershell.exe'.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used, including 'Uninstall-WindowsFeature' and 'Windows-Defender'.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the PowerShell command was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
